rxn_class_summary: Synthesis of vicinal azidoacetamides from alkenes using TMSN3, probed by a radical clock experiment with a vinylcyclopropane substrate.

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical at benzylic position of vinylcyclopropane (pre ring-opening)
ring-opened homoallyl/benzyl radical after cyclopropane fragmentation
ring-opened carbocation trapped by acetonitrile (Ritter-type) en route to acetamide

electron_flow: Addition to the styryl alkene generates a benzylic radical that triggers ring-opening of the adjacent cyclopropane to give a distal homoallyl radical; subsequent oxidation to a cation is intercepted by MeCN (Ritter) and azide delivery from TMSN3 gives the linear 1,4-azidoacetamide 31.

probe_reagents.name: 1-(2-phenylcyclopropyl)vinyl)benzene (30)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened acyclic product 31 (N-(4-azido-1,4-diphenylbut-3-en-1-yl)acetamide) isolated in 17% yield, 4.4:1 dr, consistent with a radical intermediate that opens the cyclopropane.

notes: Only the radical clock experiment is shown on this page; the ring-opened product implicates a carbon radical intermediate, but a radical-polar crossover is suggested by formation of the acetamide moiety (Ritter-type trapping of a cation by acetonitrile).
